Lepidium meyenii, also known as Maca, is a Peruvian plant that belongs to the Brassicaceae family. It has been used for centuries in traditional medicine to promote fertility, energy, and hormone balance, and its health benefits include reducing stress, improving memory, and supporting bone health. The bioactive constituents of Maca include alkaloids, glycosides, and other compounds that are responsible for its medicinal properties. Maca is commonly prepared as a powder, capsule, or tea, and is often used in herbal supplements to support reproductive health, menopause symptoms, and athletic performance.
